coverage.disclosure: Full text evaluated; no sampling-based gaps disclosed

criteria.fit: Strong eco-fable premise with interspecies love and survival ethics.
criteria.gap: Central conflict is implied more than explicitly surfaced in the early pages.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Strong eco-fable premise with interspecies love and survival ethics; central conflict could be stated more explicitly earlier.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers feel the ambition but may need clearer early framing to track stakes.
revisionRecommendations: State the main dilemma in the opening movement so readers can track how each choice bends that line.
Articulate who risks what, for whom, and against what opposition in the first key scene.

criteria.fit: Bullying, rescues, and herd politics provide real forward pull when on the page.
criteria.gap: Exposition and lore occasionally stall momentum where conflict could escalate.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Opening hook is vivid, but momentum sags when exposition and lore displace on-page pressure.
criteria.readerImpact: Some readers will admire the world while waiting longer than necessary to feel pulled through events.
revisionRecommendations: Fold essential lore into scenes where a live threat or desire is active.
Insert conflict beats before or within reflective passages rather than after them.

criteria.fit: Roles and motives are clear across Newton, Rana, Twillow, Snappy, Thorander, and elders.
criteria.gap: Key turns lack fully dramatized interior beats, softening emotional impact.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Roles and motives are clear, yet key characters need deeper interiority at decision points.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers understand choices but feel them less intensely than they could.
revisionRecommendations: Add precise sensory-thought beats at major decision points.
Let fears, desires, and beliefs surface in on-page interiority before action.

criteria.fit: A confident, sometimes mythic narrative presence suits the fable frame.
criteria.gap: Hybrid registers (comic, didactic, mythic) sometimes collide at high-stakes beats.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Distinctive, sometimes mythic voice; tonal registers occasionally clash at high-stakes moments.
criteria.readerImpact: Voice feels distinctive but can blur emotional intensity when tones mix mid-scene.
revisionRecommendations: Stabilize tone in climactic scenes around the POV character's emotional state.
Reserve register shifts for deliberate, clearly framed transitions.

criteria.fit: Many scenes have clear situation, stakes, and visible conflict.
criteria.gap: Some scenes overrun into description and exit without a sharp turn or decision.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Scenes are well-blocked and purposeful but drift into descriptive overrun instead of turning sharply.
criteria.readerImpact: Momentum inside scenes can blur, even when the underlying beats are strong.
revisionRecommendations: Compress description at apex moments.
End scenes on a decision, reveal, or sharpened question rather than soft taper.

criteria.fit: Dialogue reliably encodes role, belief, and doctrine.
criteria.gap: Characters frequently state theme and doctrine outright instead of implying it.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Role-based dialogue works, though subtext is often replaced by overt thematic statement.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers receive the ideas clearly but with less dramatic tension in conversations.
revisionRecommendations: Replace at least one overt thematic line per exchange with an image or implication.
Let disagreement and misreading between characters carry thematic weight.

criteria.fit: Ecological, moral, and social systems are coherent and meaningfully tied to plot outcomes.
criteria.gap: Some insights arrive as narrated explanation rather than embodied consequence.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Coherent ecological and moral architecture; theme is sometimes over-explained in narration.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers understand the argument but occasionally feel told rather than shown.
revisionRecommendations: Let turning points and costs dramatize the core arguments.
Use recurring motifs and consequences instead of reiterating thesis-level statements.

criteria.fit: Species dynamics, doctrine, and environmental context feel specific and internally coherent.
criteria.gap: Some survival rules and ecological constraints remain implicit where explicit rules would help.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Species dynamics and environmental logic are engaging, but survival rules and costs could be sharper.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers trust the world but may not always foresee costs or limits in advance.
revisionRecommendations: State at least one concrete rule in a live scene and immediately show its cost.
Clarify limits when characters test environmental boundaries.

criteria.fit: Prose is vivid and imaginative, with strong sensory textures.
criteria.gap: Excess description and stacked modifiers can blur focus and slow beats.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Vivid, inventive prose that can become over-described, softening focus and narrative snap.
criteria.readerImpact: Readers get a lush surface experience but may struggle to track what matters most in a moment.
revisionRecommendations: Trim descriptive density at turning points to 1–2 sharp details.
Vary sentence length to create intentional emphasis and relief.

criteria.fit: Overall arc supports a full novella-length journey.
criteria.gap: Local pacing is uneven where reflection or exposition clusters without enough live pressure.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Structural through-line is sound, yet local pacing is uneven due to long reflective or expository spans.
criteria.readerImpact: Some stretches feel heavy relative to the underlying story strength.
revisionRecommendations: Identify and trim the heaviest reflective spans; re-stage key pages with live conflict active.
Spread necessary exposition across scenes with strong situational stakes.

criteria.gap: The provided text does not fully reveal final outcomes for core promises.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Not evaluated — insufficient evidence in sample.
criteria.readerImpact: No responsible manuscript-wide closure judgment is possible from this packet.

criteria.fit: Premise and voice are distinctive and align with eco-fable / allegorical fantasy territory.
criteria.gap: Current pacing, focus, and positioning would likely hinder successful submission.
criteria.oneLineFinding: Concept is marketable, but genre positioning and execution need refinement before submission.
criteria.readerImpact: Agents/editors may see promise but also significant readiness work.
revisionRecommendations: After structural and line-level revisions, sharpen logline and comps toward a clear shelf.
Clarify whether to lean into adult eco-fantasy, literary fable, or upper-YA allegory.

topRevisionPriorities.title: Clarify and front-load the central conflict
topRevisionPriorities.rationale: State the eco-moral dilemma and Newton–Rana stakes explicitly in the opening so readers can lock onto what's at risk.

topRevisionPriorities.title: Reduce exposition and keep conflict live in the middle
topRevisionPriorities.rationale: Fold lore into scenes with active stakes; insert conflict beats before or within reflective passages to smooth pacing.

topRevisionPriorities.title: Deepen interiority at key choice-points
topRevisionPriorities.rationale: Add sensory-thought beats and emotionally specific reactions before pivotal decisions to ground arcs psychologically.

openPromises: Herd fracture and eventual reunification or new alliances
The human threat as looming external force

governanceNotes.craftVsIntelligence: CRAFT (prose, pacing, dialogue, scene construction) scores lower than INTELLIGENCE (theme, world-building, concept), which is appropriate for this manuscript. Future evaluations must preserve this distinction.
governanceNotes.closureHandling: Narrative Closure & Promises is marked INSUFFICIENT_SIGNAL. This is a deliberate governance decision: the packet does not provide full end-material, so a responsible evaluator cannot claim high confidence in closure scoring. This must not be silently converted to a low score.
governanceNotes.canonContinuity: All 13 recurring characters listed in canonicalCharacters must appear in Character Depth, World-Building, and Theme commentary. Omitting Twillow, Snappy, or Thorander is a test failure.
governanceNotes.regressionGate: Any future RevisionGrade update that produces scores outside the tolerance band (±1 per criterion) or that downgrades closure to a numeric score without full packet evidence must fail automated regression.
